solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int bestClosingTime(string customers) {
        int n = customers.length();
        int totalY = 0;
        for (char c : customers) {
            if (c == 'Y') totalY++;
        }

        int minPenalty = INT_MAX, res = 0;
        int currPenalty = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (customers[i] == 'N') currPenalty++;
            if (customers[i] == 'Y') totalY--;
            if (currPenalty + totalY < minPenalty) {
                minPenalty = currPenalty + totalY;
                res = i;
            }
        }

        if (totalY < minPenalty) {
            res = n;
        }

        return res;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution calculates the minimum penalty by iterating through the string once, maintaining a running penalty and total 'Y's. It avoids recalculating penalties from scratch for each closing time, making it efficient. The key strength is its linear time and constant space complexity. The weakness is that it requires careful tracking of penalties and indices.>
